name: SyncView Calendar — Reorder (batch)

parameters.jsCode:
const b = $('Build BatchUpdate').first().json;
return [{ json: { ok: true, updated: b.updated, skipped: b.skipped } }];

parameters.content:
## SyncView Calendar — Reorder (batch)

POST /webhook/calendar-reorder-batch — body `{ client, items: [{id, order_index}, …] }` (same contract as calendar-reorder).

Rewrites all order_index (+ updated_at) cells in ONE values:batchUpdate call — 3 fixed Google API calls total (header row → id column → batchUpdate) instead of one Sheets update per row. A 20-card drag: ~20 sequential calls → 3.

- Unknown ids are skipped and reported in `skipped` — same phantom-row guard as the original (no append, ever).
- Missing order_index column → error → FE rolls the drag back.
- Responds AFTER the write completes, so the FE serialized-reorder funnel stays race-free.

The original "SyncView Calendar — Reorder" workflow stays live as the FE fallback. Pre-change snapshot of it: repo n8n-backups/calendar-reorder.2026-06-10.pre-batch.json
